data.title: Nvidia names Nicholas Parker to lead field operations
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

200% short exposure to NVDA with NVDS

The latest announcement is out from Nvidia ( (NVDA) ).

On June 28, 2026, NVIDIA announced that Ajay K. Puri, its Executive Vice President of Worldwide Field Operations and a 21-year veteran of the company, intends to retire from his role once his successor begins, while remaining in a senior advisory position to support a smooth leadership transition. On July 1, 2026, NVIDIA appointed Nicholas Parker, a 26-year Microsoft veteran with extensive commercial and partner sales experience, as Puri’s successor, effective upon his expected employment start on August 24, 2026, under a compensation package that includes a $1 million base salary, cash incentives, and substantial equity awards tied to long-term shareholder returns, underscoring the strategic importance of the role as NVIDIA extends its AI infrastructure leadership and ecosystem.

Valuation remains premium (P/E ~29.7 with a very low dividend yield), and limited technical-indicator data plus high beta adds some risk, keeping the overall score below the top end.

Integrated BioPharma ( (INBP) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On June 30, 2026, Integrated BioPharma and Manhattan Drug Company amended their loan arrangements with PNC Bank, extending the expiration of their existing committed revolving line of credit to April 14, 2027 while maintaining the $4,000,000 borrowing limit. The amendment also introduced a new $250,000 Convertible Line of Credit Note dedicated to equipment and vehicle purchases, replacing a matured $500,000 Convertible Equipment Line of Credit and adjusting interest terms tied to daily one-month SOFR plus 250 basis points.

These changes refined the company’s capital structure by rolling over its equipment-focused borrowing facility into a smaller, convertible line of credit with flexible interest options after a defined conversion date. The revised credit profile is likely to influence how Integrated BioPharma finances asset acquisitions and manages liquidity, with potential implications for its cost of capital and operational investment planning.

KEPCO has released an English summary of its Corporate Governance Report, detailing the governance framework in place as of May 31, 2026 and aimed at improving investor understanding of its oversight systems. The company reports a governance compliance rate of 73.3% for 2025, noting partial adoption of best practices such as electronic voting and independent internal audit structures, while still lacking measures like extended AGM notice and predictable dividend policies.

The report emphasizes a board structure where eight non-standing directors outnumber seven standing directors, with the chair drawn from non-standing members to reinforce independent decision-making and transparent oversight. KEPCO also highlights four specialized board subcommittees, including an Audit Committee and an ESG Committee first established in December 2020 among Korean public enterprises, which together strengthen risk management, sustainable business strategy and director nominations, thereby shaping the company’s governance and accountability to shareholders and stakeholders.

On December 30, 2025, TAP Real Estate Technologies, Inc. entered into a license agreement with TAP, Inc. to use TAP’s token engine, blockchain registry, wallet, and related technology in the real estate sector. That original agreement was due to expire on June 30, 2026.

On June 29, 2026, the parties replaced it with a perpetual, amended and restated license that grants TAP Real Estate Technologies exclusive rights against third parties in real estate, while TAP retains its own usage rights. The company agreed to pay a total of $700,000 for the royalty-free license, of which $695,000 has already been paid and the remaining $5,000 is due by September 30, 2026, making the license fully paid up upon final payment.

On July 1, 2026, Virtuix announced that a Pennsylvania Air National Guard unit in Horsham purchased and deployed the company’s Omni One platform for immersive, AI-driven military training. The system will be used to experiment with movement-based virtual reality scenarios designed to enhance warfighter readiness, marking Virtuix’s first Air National Guard deployment and broadening its presence in U.S. defense.

The deployment follows recent wins with the U.S. Air Force, Marine Corps, Navy, Army, and West Point, including an AFWERX SBIR award, a Marine Corps VR infantry training project, and a Navy development agreement. data.title: Obsidian Energy Closes Belly River Deal and Lifts 2026 Production Outlook
data.description:

  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Obsidian Energy ( (TSE:OBE) ) has provided an announcement.

Obsidian Energy announced on July 2, 2026 that it closed its previously disclosed acquisition of Belly River light oil assets in the Wilson Creek area of Willesden Green on June 30, 2026, paying about $98 million and adding roughly 2,500 boe/d and 35 net sections of land. The deal, funded through its credit facilities, expands the scale of its Willesden Green position and is aimed at driving sustained production growth toward about 20,000 boe/d and higher free cash flow.

Following the acquisition, the company raised its 2026 average production guidance to 29,000–31,000 boe/d while keeping capital spending at $300–$325 million, and outlined a six-well program on the acquired lands in early 2027 to lift output from those assets to about 3,000 boe/d. Management now targets approximately 22% total production growth and 28% light oil growth in 2027, expecting lower operating and break-even costs and highlighting a stronger balance sheet and expanded credit facility as key support for its shareholder-focused growth plans.

On July 1, 2026, Itaú Unibanco announced that it has won a new bidding process run by the Government of the State of Minas Gerais to continue providing payment services for roughly 670,000 state employees, retirees, pensioners, and corporate suppliers. The newly executed agreement covers a five-year term starting December 22, 2026, ensuring continuity of Itaú’s role in handling the state’s payroll and reinforcing its footprint in Brazil’s public-sector financial services.

Under the new contract, Itaú Unibanco S.A., a subsidiary of the holding company, will pay a total of BRL 2.188 billion for the payroll management rights, an amount that will be booked as an intangible asset and recognized in deferred profit or loss. The deal secures a large, stable client base for the bank, potentially supporting long-term revenue streams while impacting its balance sheet through the sizable intangible asset, a development that is relevant for investors monitoring Itaú’s capital allocation and public-sector exposure.

data.description:

An announcement from POSCO ( (PKX) ) is now available.

On July 2, 2026, POSCO Holdings outlined a mid- to long-term portfolio transformation and financial strategy at its CEO Investor Day in Seoul. The group plans a “Triple-Core” portfolio built around Industrial, Strategic and Energy Resources, targeting overseas crude steel capacity of 10 million tons by 2031, lithium production of 173,000 tons by 2033 to rank among the top five global producers, and a broader footprint in rare earths, specialty gases, LNG and renewable energy.

Management set 2028 financial goals of KRW 87.9 trillion in consolidated revenue and KRW 6.7 trillion in operating profit, supported by KRW 29.1 trillion in investment from 2026 to 2028. POSCO also announced a shareholder value-focused capital policy, aiming for a 35–40% payout of adjusted net income via dividends and buybacks, reducing holding-company discounts by monetizing some listed subsidiaries, and targeting a price-to-book ratio of at least 1.0x by 2028, signaling a bid to re-rate the stock and strengthen its strategic positioning in global resource markets.

data.title: Brenmiller Energy Buys Hungarian Solar Facility to Launch BNRG360 Clean-Energy Hub
data.description:

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Brenmiller ( (BNRG) ) just unveiled an update.

On July 2, 2026, Brenmiller Energy announced the purchase of a 1.2 MWp photovoltaic facility in Hungary located next to its planned industrial energy project with Partner in Pet Food Hungaria. The approximately $1.1 million acquisition is expected to add about $173,000 in average annual revenue and immediately supplies renewable electricity to the grid while the company prepares to build out a larger integrated energy hub.

The deal marks Brenmiller’s first execution of its BNRG360 strategy and signals a shift from being primarily a thermal energy storage equipment provider to owning and operating clean-energy assets that generate recurring income. By transforming the site into a multi-technology platform combining up to 20 MW of solar, battery and thermal storage with direct industrial power and heat supply, Brenmiller aims to strengthen its Energy-as-a-Service model, enhance industrial decarbonization solutions, and broaden its portfolio of income-producing infrastructure for shareholders.

Brenmiller Energy Ltd. is a clean energy company specializing in proprietary thermal energy storage solutions for industrial and utility customers, anchored by its patented bGen platform and broader BNRG360 integrated heat-and-power strategy. Listed on Nasdaq under the ticker BNRG, the Israel-based firm focuses on decarbonizing industrial energy use by combining renewable generation, storage and long-term Energy-as-a-Service offerings.

Through its evolving role as a developer, owner and operator of clean energy infrastructure, Brenmiller targets recurring, service-based revenues rather than one-off equipment sales. Integrated Media Technology ( (IMTE) ) has issued an announcement.

Integrated Media Technology Limited has announced changes to its corporate governance structure, centered on the role of Company Secretary. The Australia‑based technology and media company continues to rely on seasoned financial and advisory expertise within its board to support its compliance and reporting obligations as a foreign private issuer in the U.S. market.

On July 1, 2026, the board terminated the contract of Company Secretary Julie Ann Edwards, stating the decision did not stem from any disagreement over the company’s operations, policies or practices. The firm’s leadership structure emphasizes strong financial oversight, with roles such as Company Secretary central to managing its reporting obligations and interface with regulators. data.title: Trinity Biotech Clinical Data Validates CGM+ Advantage in Tackling False Low Glucose Alerts
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Trinity Biotech ( (TRIB) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 2, 2026, Trinity Biotech reported clinical trial results from a pre-pivotal study of its next-generation CGM+ wearable biosensor platform in insulin-dependent people with diabetes. Analysis of about 5,000 hours of wear time showed CGM+ can algorithmically distinguish nocturnal compression-related false low glucose readings from true blood glucose lows by combining glucose data with additional physiological signals.

The company argues that this multi-sensor capability addresses a major limitation of conventional glucose-only CGM systems, which can generate sleep-disrupting false alarms and drive avoidable glucose variability, with knock-on effects for automated insulin delivery decisions. Management says the findings validate CGM+’s platform strategy, strengthen its differentiation in the roughly $15 billion global CGM market, and will be incorporated as the product advances toward pivotal trials and regulatory submission.

Spark’s Take on TRIB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TRIB is a Neutral.

TRIB scores low primarily due to very weak financial performance (multi-year losses, negative equity, rising debt, and ongoing cash burn). Valuation is also constrained because the company is unprofitable (negative P/E) and there is no dividend yield data to offset risk.

To see Spark’s full report on TRIB stock,
click here.

More about Trinity Biotech

Trinity Biotech plc is a commercial-stage biotechnology company focused on human diagnostics and diabetes management solutions, including wearable biosensors. It develops, acquires, manufactures, and markets diagnostic systems for point-of-care and clinical laboratory segments, and has expanded into CGM and advanced liquid cooling for AI data centers through acquisitions and its Trinovium subsidiary.

The company sells directly in the U.S. and via international distributors and strategic partners in over 75 countries worldwide. Its CGM+ platform targets the global continuous glucose monitoring market, seeking differentiation with multi-sensor, AI-driven analytics that add physiological context to traditional glucose data.

data.title: Integrated Media Technology Wins Nasdaq Extension to Meet Minimum Bid Price Rule
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest announcement is out from Integrated Media Technology ( (IMTE) ).

On June 30, 2026, Integrated Media Technology Limited disclosed that it had received notice from Nasdaq granting an additional 180 days, until December 29, 2026, to regain compliance with the exchange’s minimum bid price requirement. This extension followed an earlier December 30, 2025 notification that the company’s stock had traded below the $1 minimum for 30 consecutive business days through December 23, 2025.

Although Integrated Media Technology did not restore its share price to the required level during the initial compliance window that ended June 29, 2026, Nasdaq determined the company remained eligible for a second 180-day period. To maintain its listing, the firm’s stock must close at or above $1 for at least ten consecutive business days within this new timeframe, and management plans to evaluate available options to achieve this, while trading in IMTE shares continues uninterrupted on Nasdaq.

More about Integrated Media Technology

Integrated Media Technology Limited is an Australia-based company listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker IMTE. The firm operates within the broader technology and media space, with its common stock subject to U.S. exchange listing standards that govern minimum bid price and continued market eligibility.

As a foreign private issuer, Integrated Media Technology reports to U.S. regulators under Form 20-F and related filings. Its shares are currently traded on Nasdaq, and the company’s compliance with listing rules is closely monitored by the Nasdaq Listing Qualifications department, reflecting its reliance on U.S. capital markets.

Surf Air Mobility, Inc. ( (SRFM) ) has provided an update.

On June 26, 2026, Surf Air Mobility announced an electric aircraft demonstration program in Hawaiʻi with BETA Technologies, using BETA’s ALIA CTOL aircraft over six to eight weeks to gather operational, economic and infrastructure data. Hawaiian Airlines is supporting the trial with route insights and feasibility work, and Surf Air Mobility later corrected its release to clarify that it plans, rather than has already managed, to be the first Part 135 operator to commercialize electric passenger flights for scheduled and on-demand services.

The program leverages Surf Air’s Mokulele Airlines network, existing Hawaiʻi airport infrastructure and SurfOS software to assess how electric aircraft could support future cargo and passenger operations on the state’s interisland routes. On July 1, 2026, Surf Air Mobility also closed an exchange of its existing $46.9 million senior secured convertible note into two new secured notes due 2027 and 2028, reshaping its debt profile as it commits capital and operational focus to scaling commercial electric aviation in Hawaiʻi.

These capabilities position Surf Air Mobility as a player in regional and commuter aviation, with a particular focus on integrating electric aircraft into short-haul networks.
